sql_array: CREATE TABLE sales(id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), sale_date DATE);
CREATE TABLE inventory(item_id INT, quantity INT, last_updated DATE);

SELECT item_id, median(amount) OVER (PARTITION BY item_id) AS median_amount FROM (SELECT amount, sales.id AS item_id FROM sales LEFT JOIN inventory ON sales.id = inventory.item_id WHERE inventory.quantity IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
SELECT sale_date, median(amount - 100) FROM sales WHERE amount IS NOT NULL GROUP BY sale_date HAVING median(amount - 100) < 0;
SELECT median(COALESCE(quantity, 0)) - INTERVAL 5 DAY AS adjusted_median FROM inventory;
SELECT DATE '2023-01-16' - INTERVAL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sales WHERE amount IS NOT NULL) DAY AS reduced_date;
SELECT median(amount) OVER (ORDER BY amount ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) AS overall_median, sale_date FROM sales WHERE sale_date > (DATE '2023-01-12' - INTERVAL 3 DAY);

SELECT item_id, median(COALESCE(amount, 0)) FROM sales LEFT JOIN inventory ON sales.id = inventory.item_id WHERE inventory.last_updated < DATE '2023-01-15' GROUP BY item_id HAVING median(COALESCE(amount, 0)) IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE test_dates(date_col DATE, value DECIMAL(10,2));
SELECT value - 50 AS modified_value FROM test_dates WHERE date_col < (SELECT DATE '2023-12-31' - INTERVAL 2 DAY);
SELECT median((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sales) - amount) AS adjusted_median FROM sales;
